task_completion.description: Locate and open the original quote
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the original quote named 'Q3 Hardware Refresh' being opened or viewed in the CRM interface.

task_completion.description: Initiate the clone or duplicate action
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking a 'Clone', 'Duplicate', or similar action button, resulting in a new quote form that is pre-filled with the original quote's data.
Look for the Subject text field being modified to exactly 'Q3 Hardware Refresh - Revision 1'.
Look for the Quote Stage dropdown or field being changed to 'Reviewed'.
Look for the overall discount field in the item details or pricing section being set to 10 or 10%.
Look for a success message or the view of the newly saved quote record displaying the updated subject ('Q3 Hardware Refresh - Revision 1'), stage ('Reviewed'), and 10% discount.

integrity.description: Agent used the CRM graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent interacts with the actual CRM web interface to perform the tasks, rather than using a terminal, database client, or script.

integrity.description: Agent cloned the quote instead of creating from scratch
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent uses the system's clone/duplicate feature. The new quote should automatically contain the original items (e.g., 'Enterprise Server Model X') without the agent manually adding them.
Ensure the agent does not simply edit and save over the original 'Q3 Hardware Refresh' quote. The action must clearly be a clone/duplicate resulting in a new record.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original quote to be cloned is named 'Q3 Hardware Refresh'. The new cloned quote must have the Subject 'Q3 Hardware Refresh - Revision 1', the Quote Stage set to 'Reviewed', and an overall discount of 10% applied. The original quote must remain unmodified (i.e., the agent must use a clone/duplicate feature, not just edit the original).
